---
title: "CPI deflation and the release-basis vintage"
subtitle: "Why some figures deflate to December-2025 dollars and others keep nominal prices — and how to tell which is which."
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
---

## What is being measured

When a figure on this site says it shows **real dollars**, it means nominal prices have been divided by a Consumer Price Index value to remove the effect of general price-level change. The CPI series used is the **CPI-U, All Items, U.S. City Average, Not Seasonally Adjusted** ([BLS series `CUUR0000SA0`](https://data.bls.gov/timeseries/CUUR0000SA0); see also the [BLS Handbook of Methods, CPI chapter](https://www.bls.gov/opub/hom/cpi/home.htm) for the underlying construction).

NSA is used rather than seasonally adjusted CPI because seasonal-adjustment patterns reflect a general consumer basket and do not cleanly transfer to livestock prices; the actual price level a producer faced in a given month is what NSA reports.

## Where the data comes from

- **Series**: [`CUUR0000SA0`](https://data.bls.gov/timeseries/CUUR0000SA0)
- **Publisher**: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics, [public v2 API](https://www.bls.gov/developers/api_signature_v2.htm)
- **License**: U.S. public domain ([17 U.S.C. § 105](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/17/105))
- **Pipeline**: [`pipelines/bls/`](https://github.com/lma-explorer/lma-explorer.github.io/tree/main/pipelines/bls)
- **Schedule**: 16th of each month, 14:00 UTC ([`bls_cpi_refresh.yml`](https://github.com/lma-explorer/lma-explorer.github.io/blob/main/.github/workflows/bls_cpi_refresh.yml))

The pipeline writes two artifacts that figures choose between:

```
data/processed/cpi_latest.parquet            # newest vintage, updated monthly
data/processed/cpi_release_basis_2025.parquet  # one-time release artifact,
                                             # a CPI vintage pinned to the
                                             # December 2025 basis month
                                             # (committed once, never
                                             # overwritten by the pipeline)
```

The release-basis file is not yet committed — it will be created at Phase 1 release as a frozen reference vintage. Until then, figures on this site deflate with `cpi_latest.parquet` using December 2025 as the basis month. When the release-basis file lands, figures that need to reproduce platform-baseline real-dollar numbers exactly will switch their read-path to it, so historical chart values stay stable across BLS revisions.

## Current freshness

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| label: bls-freshness

from pathlib import Path
import json
import pandas as pd
from IPython.display import Markdown

MANIFEST = Path("..") / "data" / "processed" / "MANIFEST.json"
if MANIFEST.exists():
    with MANIFEST.open("r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        m = json.load(f)
    entries = m.get("entries", [])
    if entries:
        e = entries[-1]
        freshness = (
            f"- **Latest vintage committed**: `{e.get('vintage','—')}`  \n"
            f"- **Rows in the snapshot**: {e.get('rows','—'):,}  \n"
            f"- **SHA-256**: `{e.get('sha256','—')[:16]}…`  \n"
            f"- **Written (UTC)**: {e.get('written_at_utc','—')}  \n"
            f"- **Total vintages committed to date**: {len(entries)}"
        )
    else:
        freshness = "_No vintages committed yet. The next scheduled BLS refresh on the 16th at 14:00 UTC will populate this._"
else:
    freshness = "_Manifest not yet created. Expected path: `data/processed/MANIFEST.json`._"
Markdown(freshness)
```

## The CPI-U series visualized

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| label: cpi-series

from pathlib import Path
import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go

CPI_LATEST = Path("..") / "data" / "processed" / "cpi_latest.parquet"

if CPI_LATEST.exists():
    cpi = pd.read_parquet(CPI_LATEST)
    cpi["period"] = pd.to_datetime(cpi["period"])
    cpi = cpi.sort_values("period")

    fig = go.Figure()
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=cpi["period"], y=cpi["cpi_u"],
        mode="lines", line=dict(color="#1a1a1a", width=2),
        hovertemplate="<b>%{x|%B %Y}</b><br>CPI-U (NSA): %{y:.3f}<extra></extra>",
        name="CUUR0000SA0",
    ))
    # Mark the December 2025 basis
    basis_row = cpi[cpi["period"] == pd.Timestamp("2025-12-01")]
    if not basis_row.empty:
        basis_val = float(basis_row["cpi_u"].iloc[0])
        fig.add_hline(
            y=basis_val, line_dash="dot", line_color="#d17a00",
            annotation_text=f"December 2025 basis = {basis_val:.2f}",
            annotation_position="top left",
        )
    fig.update_layout(
        height=360,
        margin=dict(t=40, b=40, l=70, r=20),
        paper_bgcolor="white", plot_bgcolor="white",
        xaxis=dict(title_text="Month", showgrid=False, linecolor="#888", ticks="outside"),
        yaxis=dict(title_text="CPI-U (1982-84 = 100)", showgrid=True, gridcolor="#e5e5e5", linecolor="#888", ticks="outside"),
        font=dict(family="Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif", size=12, color="#222"),
        hovermode="x unified",
    )
    fig.show()
else:
    print("CPI parquet not yet committed. The first BLS refresh will write it.")
```

## The two-vintage choice

BLS revises CPI values. A figure that is meant to reproduce a fixed historical comparison must keep deflating with the CPI vintage that was current when the comparison was first published (December 2025 basis on this site), even after BLS revises later — otherwise yesterday's chart and today's chart can disagree about a 2019 price for no reason other than a CPI revision in between. A figure that is meant to show current real prices should deflate with the most recent vintage. We do not let either choice be an implicit default.

**Every `.qmd` file that deflates names its CPI vintage in its first code cell.** It is deliberate and visible in the source. The page caption reports the vintage too, so a reader can see at a glance which basis is in play.

## How the deflator is actually computed

For any observation made in month *m*, the real price in December-2025 dollars is:

$$
p_{\text{real},m} = p_{\text{nominal},m} \times \frac{\text{CPI}_{\text{Dec 2025}}}{\text{CPI}_m}
$$

Each auction observation carries a `report_date`; we floor that date to the first of the month, look up the CPI value for that month, and compute the deflator row-by-row. Aggregations (weekly means, weight-class means) operate on the deflated column, not on a single aggregate deflator — because a weighted mean of real prices is not equal to the nominal mean times a mean deflator when the data spans multiple months.

## The month-over-month sanity check

Before any new vintage is snapshotted, the pipeline checks that no month-over-month CPI change exceeds 5 percent in absolute value. CPI has not moved that fast even in the 2021–2022 inflation spike; a change beyond that bound is almost certainly a unit shift, a decimal error, or a malformed API response. If the check fails, the pipeline exits non-zero, opens a `data-source-drift` issue, and the site keeps serving the prior vintage.

BLS also publishes *suppression markers* (empty strings, dashes, `(NA)`) for cells that are unavailable or preliminary. The validator treats these as missing rather than as failures; a single suppressed month never crashes the pipeline.

## How to read the vintage stamp

When a figure caption says **Deflation basis: December 2025 CPI-U NSA**, that means the deflation used BLS's value for December 2025 as the numerator of the deflator. The specific BLS publication month the value came from is in the manifest; see the "Current freshness" section above for the latest vintage stamp.

If you are reproducing a figure locally and BLS has since revised the December 2025 value, your numbers will differ slightly — which is why the site publishes the vintage alongside the figure rather than burying it in code.

## What is deliberately left out

- **Regional CPIs** (e.g., CPI for the Mountain Census region) are not used. A producer in New Mexico might prefer a regional deflator; the site keeps CPI-U U.S. City Average because it has the deepest continuous monthly history of any BLS price index, is the index BLS itself flags as the headline All-Items series, and is widely used as a deflator across producer-facing economic reporting. Regional alternatives are a Phase 2 candidate.
- **Chained CPI-U (C-CPI-U)** is not used. CPI-U has the longer continuous history (the chained series begins in December 1999) and is the more common reference in producer-facing livestock-price reporting.
- **Producer Price Index (PPI)** is not used for livestock-price deflation here. A separate Phase 2 methodology page will introduce PPI for feed-cost figures where it is more appropriate.
- **Seasonal-adjustment** (the SA variant of CPI-U) is not used — see the opening paragraph.

## Code

- Pipeline source: [`pipelines/bls/`](https://github.com/lma-explorer/lma-explorer.github.io/tree/main/pipelines/bls)
- This page's source: [`site/methodology/cpi.qmd`](https://github.com/lma-explorer/lma-explorer.github.io/blob/main/site/methodology/cpi.qmd)
- Workflow file: [`.github/workflows/bls_cpi_refresh.yml`](https://github.com/lma-explorer/lma-explorer.github.io/blob/main/.github/workflows/bls_cpi_refresh.yml)

## Contact

Spotted an error or a methodological disagreement? Open a [new issue](https://github.com/lma-explorer/lma-explorer.github.io/issues/new/choose) with the `methodology` label.
